# SovTech and the Bigger Picture {.appendix}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "SovTech extends SupTech and RegTech principles to sovereign debt management."
flowchart LR
    A["SupTech / RegTech\n(financial supervision)"] --> B["SovTech\n(sovereign debt management)"]
    B --> C["Data pipelines"]
    B --> D["Analytical tools"]
    B --> E["Reporting interfaces"]
    B --> F["Contract transparency"]
```

**SovTech** is an open source initiative to bring the same technology-driven approach that transformed financial supervision (SupTech) to sovereign debt management. It is not a single tool. It is an approach to building tools: modular components that work independently but interoperate.

**The problem.** Debt management offices face growing analytical demands (climate scenarios, stress testing, evolving creditor landscapes) but still rely on Excel, legacy systems, and one-off consulting models. The sovereign clause corpus is one component of a broader toolkit.

**Design principles.**

| Principle | What it means |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Modularity** | Components work like building blocks. Adopt what you need without wholesale system replacement. |
| **Open source** | MIT licensed. Anyone can examine, adapt, and extend. Transparency matters for institutions that must defend their methodologies. |
| **AI as enabler** | Eliminate analytical toil (document processing, data reconciliation). Free analysts to focus on the hard problems. Human-in-the-loop verification for trust. |

**Who.** [Teal Insights](https://tealinsights.com) builds the tools, with support from [NatureFinance](https://www.naturefinance.net/). The approach draws on NatureFinance's experience building SupTech solutions for financial regulators in emerging markets, including co-authoring the BIS foundational paper "The SupTech Generations" (di Castri, Kulenkampff, Hohl, and Prenio, 2019).

**Climate connection.** Sovereign debt terms affect restructuring capacity, which affects climate investment. Better contract transparency supports better debt sustainability analysis.

**What's next.** A paper on SovTech has been proposed for the 4th Public Debt Management Conference in Paris (September 2026). This corpus is a proof of concept for one piece of the puzzle.

All code: [GitHub](https://github.com/Teal-Insights/sovereign-prospectus-corpus)
